# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

# Build stage 1: Get python 2.7.14
FROM python:2.7-slim AS build-stage1
 
# Set the working directory to /app/
WORKDIR /app

 # Copy the current directory contents into the container at /app/
COPY . ./

# install pip requirements 
RUN pip --no-cache-dir install -r requirements.txt

# Build stage 2: Get python 3.5.4 
FROM python:3.7 AS build-stage1
 
# Set the working directory to /app/
WORKDIR /app

 # Copy the current directory contents into the container at /app/
COPY . ./

# install pip requirements 
RUN pip --no-cache-dir install -r requirements.txt
